No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to the eradication of poverty or its related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to ending hunger or improving food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to health outcomes or well-being initiatives.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a form of tertiary education (Target 4.3) and explicitly develops 'stronger critical thinking skills' and 'oral fluency,' which are relevant skills for employment and lifelong learning (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: The course aims to build 'greater confidence to speak out and express your point of view,' which can indirectly empower women's full and effective participation in decision-making (Target 5.5).
evidence: greater confidence to speak out and express your point of view

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to energy access or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops critical thinking and oral communication skills, which are broadly applicable and contribute indirectly to achieving productive employment and decent work (Target 8.5).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to industry, innovation, or infrastructure development.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course empowers students to 'express your point of view' and 'argue your position clearly,' fostering skills essential for social and political inclusion for all (Target 10.2).
evidence: Argue your position clearly and effectively

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable urban development or community resilience.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable consumption or production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to climate change mitigation or adaptation.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems or ocean conservation.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course directly develops skills in analyzing policy positions and arguing effectively, which are crucial for participatory decision-making and building effective, accountable, and transparent institutions (Targets 16.6, 16.7).
evidence: Argue your position clearly and effectively

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The 'Pro-Am Tournament' explicitly involves 'novice debaters matched up with more experienced debaters from outside the class,' directly fostering multi-stakeholder partnerships and knowledge sharing (Targets 17.16, 17.17).
evidence: novice debaters will get matched up with more experienced debaters from outside the class
